tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: While f2p tests are text-based (checking source content), the p2p tests actually compile the Kotlin sources (test_main_sources_compile, test_extras_property_test_compiles, test_all_sources_compile) and run JUnit tests (test_extras_property_tests_pass, test_extras_tests_pass, etc.). The combination of text checks for removal and compilation/execution tests provides behavioral verification.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests check that specific deprecated code patterns are absent from source files. A stub/no-op wouldn't pass because the original files contain the deprecated code. The agent must actively remove the deprecated APIs and migrate tests. The p2p compilation and test execution tests further ensure the changes are correct.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/kotlin (the REPO path) as a git repo, and solve.sh applies a git patch. The agent could potentially read the solution/solve.sh if it exists in the container, or could inspect git log/reflog. Also, the Dockerfile downloads files from a specific commit (BASE_COMMIT=95085a77af52e78028044f0ea5404803232ad697) and the PR reference (JetBrains/kotlin#5809) is in the test file comments, which an agent could use to look up the exact solution.
